message:
fix: isolate useSearchParams to prevent SSR bailout

CRITICAL FIX: The AnalyticsProvider was using useSearchParams() which
caused Next.js to bail out of server-side rendering for the entire app.
This resulted in pages appearing blank until JavaScript loaded.

Solution: Moved all useSearchParams usage into a separate AnalyticsTracker
component wrapped in its own Suspense boundary. This allows the main
content to SSR properly while analytics tracking loads client-side.

The symptom was pages flashing styled content then going blank, or
showing blank then loading - classic SSR bailout behavior.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
